item.title: U.S. launches back-to-back waves of strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. launched two waves of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, marking the fifth day of attacks against the country as the war ramps back up. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Senior defense officials looking at Cuba military options
item.description: Military planners have examined options for possible action against Cuba, including an Army-led air assault involving the 101st Airborne Division.

item.title: U.S. launches 2nd round of Iran strikes amid battle over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. announced two sets of strikes on Iran on Wednesday, as the war ramps back up over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: NYPD officer says Brooklyn Bridge rescue happened on 3rd day with elite unit
item.description: Cristian Yepes was on his first week with the NYPD's elite Emergency Service Unit when he helped rescue a woman on the Brooklyn Bridge.

item.title: Sen. Schiff and Blanche argue about conflicts of interest, Trump's IRS settlement
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff and acting Attorney General Todd Blanche argued over President Trump's IRS settlement during Blanche's Senate confirmation hearing on Wednesday. See the exchange.

item.title: Blanche reiterates "anti-weaponization" fund is "dead" at confirmation hearing
item.description: "It is a moot issue, meaning there is no weaponization fund. The weaponization fund is dead," Blanche told lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Warsh vows to tackle inflation in first testimony as Fed chairman
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh told the House Financial Services Committee that the central bank has "no tolerance for persistently elevated inflation."

item.title: Highlights from Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing for director of national intelligence
item.description: Jay Clayton faced intense criticism as he sought Senate confirmation to become director of national intelligence. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Why is Zelenskyy shaking up his cabinet amid successes against Russia?
item.description: Ukraine's president is shaking up his government just as observers note positive changes in the trajectory of the war with Russia.

item.title: Takeaways from Todd Blanche confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appeared before senators for five hours Wednesday as he made his case for confirmation to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity. He faced questioning over his handling of the Jeffrey Epstein case, President Trump's anti-weaponization fund and more during his Senate confirmation hearing on Wednesday. CBS News' senior Justice Department reporter Sarah Lynch reports.

item.title: Key moments from Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing for top intelligence post
item.description: President Trump's nominee for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, testified before a Senate panel as part of his confirmation process. Taurean Small shares key takeaways from the hearing.

item.title: Booker grills Todd Blanche about Ghislaine Maxwell meeting, Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: New Jersey Democratic Sen. Corey Booker grilled Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche about his meeting with Jeffrey Epstein ally and convicted criminal Ghislaine Maxwell during a Senate panel hearing on Capitol Hill on Wednesday. Booker also pressed Blanche about attending a dinner hosted by David Ellison, the CEO of Paramount Skydance, as a Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger bid continues. CBS News is owned by Paramount Skydance.

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan plan to tackle Social Security insolvency
item.description: The Promise Act would establish a legislative procedure with the goal of preserving Social Security's trust funds for the next 50 years.

item.title: Trump overturns pause of ICE vehicle stops implemented after deadly shootings
item.description: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Key moments as Todd Blanche testifies before Senate panel for attorney general confirmation
item.description: Lawmakers pressed Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche on a slew of issues during the first part of his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns breaks down the key moments, which included questions about the Epstein files and the proposed "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Here's what the Common Cents Act means for businesses, consumers
item.description: New law aims to address how businesses and consumers should transact amid the phase-out of the penny.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Clayton faces questions about election integrity, vows to enhance trust in DNI
item.description: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: U.S. Mint to make $1 gold coin with Trump's face on it
item.description: It's the latest effort by President Trump to make his mark on Washington, D.C.

item.title: Officer who rescued woman on Brooklyn Bridge was in first week on elite unit
item.description: Officer Cristian Yepes helped to save a woman's life on the Brooklyn Bridge last week. It was only his third day on the job with the NYPD Emergency Service Unit. Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Hilton employee who sent racial slur to WNBA star is fired
item.description: Hilton Grand Vacations said it has fired an employee who sent a racial slur to Las Vegas Aces guard Chelsea Gray.

item.title: Alzheimer's blood test may show who's likely to develop it in 5 to 10 years
item.description: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.

item.title: JD and Usha Vance on faith and family
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, Usha, are expecting their fourth child. They talk about family; his book, "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith"; and his future.

item.title: Second lady Usha Vance announces she is pregnant with fourth child
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance announced they are expecting their fourth child.

item.title: Usha Vance to visit Greenland
item.description: The trip was criticized Monday by Greenland Prime Minister Mute Egede, who said Washington was interfering in Greenland's internal affairs.

item.title: Vance joining wife Usha on upcoming trip to Greenland
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said he "didn't want her to have all that fun by herself."

item.title: JD Vance says his wife advised him to "trust in the wisdom" of women on their vote
item.description: Sen. JD Vance, the Republican candidate for vice president, noted that his wife, Usha, is his "top adviser," and outlined his pitch to women voters.

item.title: Who is Usha Vance, JD Vance's wife who influenced who he is today?
item.description: Republican vice-presidential nominee JD Vance wrote in his memoir that he "couldn't do it without Usha," his wife.

item.title: Have a Capital One credit card? Take these 5 steps
item.description: If you're a Capital One customer, consider adding extra levels of security to keep scammers from misusing your data
